pending_diagnosis: DIAGNOSIS: Anti-NMDA Receptor Encephalitis THE SMOKING GUN: Ovarian Teratoma found on Pelvic Ultrasound PATHOPHYSIOLOGY: This paraneoplastic syndrome occurs when the body produces IgG antibodies against the GluN1 subunit of the NMDA receptor. In approximately 50 percent of young female cases, the trigger is a germ cell tumor, most commonly a mature or immature teratoma. These tumors contain ectopic neural tissue that expresses NMDA receptors; the immune system recognizes this tissue as foreign, breaks self-tolerance, and generates antibodies that cross the blood-brain barrier. These antibodies cause the internalization and depletion of NMDA receptors on the surface of neurons, leading to a catastrophic loss of excitatory neurotransmission that manifests as psychosis, catatonia, and autonomic instability.
